title: Bank of America upgrades customer service employee tool
excerpt: Generative AI capabilities added to EricaAssist will allow customer service employees to handle client inquiries more quickly, the lender said.
content_html:
This story was originally published on Banking Dive. To receive daily news and insights, subscribe to our free daily Banking Dive newsletter.

Dive Brief:

  • Generative artificial intelligence capabilities added to Bank of America's EricaAssist, an AI agent built to aid employees in their interactions with customers, now allow it to make recommendations in real time, the bank said Tuesday.

  • The Charlotte, North Carolina-based lender said the enhancements will provide personalized guidance within a few seconds, addressing customer needs more quickly.

  • "It's really about helping our employees better serve the clients and do it more efficiently and effectively," said Ashley Ross, head of consumer client experience and business transformation at BofA, in an interview. "Anything that makes the engagement smoother for our clients and our associates is a win."

Dive Insight:

While Erica is BofA's customer-facing AI-powered virtual assistant, EricaAssist is used by about 18,000 bank customer service employees as they interact with clients. 

The AI agent runs as a desktop widget on an employee's computer and works alongside staff, helping them access information in seconds, Ross said. EricaAssist shortens average call times by almost one minute per interaction, according to the bank.

EricaAssist tells an employee why the customer is calling and their tenure at BofA, among other details, and employees can ask the AI agent questions while they help customers.

New generative AI-powered capabilities that are now live "assist them specifically in that moment with what the best next recommendation could be," said Tom Ellis, chief information officer and head of consumer technology at Bank of America, in an interview. 

That's where the bank expects to see "tangible benefits" in resolution time and customer experience, he added.

Before, customer service employees had to multitask, listening to customers while searching for policies, procedures and next steps, Ellis said. 

Now, "we have more capabilities to understand what's going on in the conversation," Ellis said, "and then to take that conversation, summarize what's going on, and then make recommendations back."

BofA plans to add more capabilities to EricaAssist, and the bank incorporates employee feedback in executing those plans, Ross said. 

The $3.5 trillion-asset bank plans to use EricaAssist to support additional servicing scenarios and business lines later this year. The lender spends about $14 billion annually on technology, with about $4 billion of that going toward new initiatives including AI. 

"Giving these advanced capabilities to our associates is now helping Ashley and I think about where we go next with digital," Ellis said.

content_html_zh:
这个故事最初发表在 Banking Dive 上。 要接收每日新闻和见解，请订阅我们免费的每日 Banking Dive 时事通讯。

潜水简介：

  • 美国银行周二表示，EricaAssist 是一款人工智能代理，旨在帮助员工与客户互动，该公司添加了生成人工智能功能，现在可以实时提出建议。
  • 这家总部位于北卡罗来纳州夏洛特的银行表示，这些增强功能将在几秒钟内提供个性化指导，从而更快地满足客户需求。
  • 美国银行消费者客户体验和业务转型主管阿什利·罗斯（Ashley Ross）在接受采访时表示：“这实际上是为了帮助我们的员工更好地为客户服务，并更高效、更有效地服务。” “任何能让我们的客户和员工的参与更加顺畅的事情都是一场胜利。”

潜水洞察力：

Erica 是美国银行面向客户的人工智能虚拟助理，而 EricaAssist 则被大约 18,000 名银行客户服务员工在与客户互动时使用。

罗斯说，人工智能代理作为员工计算机上的桌面小部件运行，并与员工一起工作，帮助他们在几秒钟内访问信息。 据该银行称，EricaAssist 将每次互动的平均通话时间缩短了近一分钟。

EricaAssist 会告诉员工客户打电话的原因以及他们在美国银行的任期等详细信息，员工可以在帮助客户时向人工智能代理提出问题。

美国银行首席信息官兼消费者技术主管汤姆·埃利斯（Tom Ellis）在接受采访时表示，现已上线的新的生成式人工智能功能“可以在那一刻专门帮助他们提出下一个最佳建议”。

他补充说，这就是银行希望在解决时间和客户体验方面看到“切实好处”的地方。

埃利斯说，以前，客户服务员工必须同时处理多项任务，一边倾听客户的意见，一边寻找政策、程序和后续步骤。

现在，“我们有更多的能力来了解对话中发生的情况，”埃利斯说，“然后进行对话，总结发生的情况，然后提出建议。”

罗斯表示，美国银行计划为 EricaAssist 添加更多功能，并且该银行在执行这些计划时会考虑员工的反馈。

这家资产达 3.5 万亿美元的银行计划在今年晚些时候使用 EricaAssist 来支持其他服务场景和业务线。 该银行每年在技术上花费约 140 亿美元，其中约 40 亿美元用于包括人工智能在内的新举措。

埃利斯说：“为我们的员工提供这些先进的功能现在正在帮助阿什利和我思考我们下一步的数字化方向。”
